mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy stance, which tends to anchor long-term interest rates and prevent significant upward pressure on the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound / accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield shows a positive direction in more than 60% of 20-day periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the average yield change is positive and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold indicates a strengthening labor market and economic recovery, leading to expectations of higher future growth and inflation, which drives the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE falls below 6.5%, the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield does not show a positive direction in more than 55% of subsequent 20-day periods, or the average yield change is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong GDP growth and accelerating consumer price inflation signal an overheating economy, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher real rates, which collectively push the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield higher over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 3% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 as of t-6mo, CPIAUCSL as of t-1mo
failure_condition: In periods where both GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL growth conditions are met, the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield shows a negative direction in more than 50% of subsequent 60-day periods.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of elevated inflation, the market anticipates central bank action to curb price increases, leading to an immediate upward adjustment in long-term interest rates and thus a positive direction for the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL is above 260, the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield does not show a positive direction in more than 50% of subsequent 5-day periods, or the average yield change is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month increase in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending and strong economic momentum, which typically translates into higher growth expectations and upward pressure on the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Following a month-over-month RSAFS growth exceeding 1.5%, the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield shows a negative direction in more than 55% of subsequent 20-day periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins economic growth and inflation expectations, leading to a gradual upward trend in the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield shows a negative direction in more than 50% of 60-day periods when PAYEMS exhibits positive month-over-month growth, or the average yield change is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently strong industrial production and robust housing starts signal broad-based economic strength and increasing productive capacity, which drives expectations for future growth and inflation, pushing the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND HOUST > 1.5 million units (annualized)
failure_condition: When both INDPRO growth and HOUST conditions are met, the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield shows a negative direction in more than 50% of subsequent 20-day periods.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate above a very low threshold signals the Federal Reserve's intent to begin normalizing monetary policy, leading to an immediate market repricing of future interest rates and a positive direction for the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25%, the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield does not show a positive direction in more than 55% of subsequent 5-day periods, or the average yield change is negative.
